description:
You are a database reliability engineer validating that the backup scheduler is correctly configured.

A configuration file has already been placed at the absolute path
/home/user/backups/conf/db_backup.ini

Its [schedule] section looks like this (do **not** copy-paste; you must read the file from disk):

[schedule]
daily_time = 02:30
weekly_day = Sun
weekly_time = 03:00

Your job is to:
1. Read /home/user/backups/conf/db_backup.ini.
2. Extract the value of the key “weekly_time” that appears inside the [schedule] section.
3. Create a **new** directory (if it does not yet exist) called /home/user/backups/logs.
4. Inside that directory create a new file named /home/user/backups/logs/next_weekly_backup.log that contains **exactly one line** in the following format (include the trailing newline, and nothing else—no leading/trailing spaces):
WEEKLY_BACKUP_TIME=03:00\n

5. After creating the file, output its contents to the terminal so the operator can visually confirm it.

The automated test will verify both:
• that /home/user/backups/logs/next_weekly_backup.log exists,  
• and that its byte-for-byte contents are precisely the single line shown above (with one UNIX LF at the end and no additional whitespace).

Use any single terminal command or short command pipeline you like—as long as the final state matches the specification above. You do not need root privileges.
truth:
Initial state:
1. Directory /home/user/backups/conf already exists with mode 755.
2. File /home/user/backups/conf/db_backup.ini exists and contains the exact 57-byte sequence (UNIX line endings):
[schedule]\n
daily_time = 02:30\n
weekly_day = Sun\n
weekly_time = 03:00\n

3. Directory /home/user/backups/logs does NOT exist at start.

Required final state for validation:
1. Directory /home/user/backups/logs present (mode 755 or 775 acceptable).
2. File /home/user/backups/logs/next_weekly_backup.log exists and its entire contents are:
WEEKLY_BACKUP_TIME=03:00\n
(exactly 23 bytes, last character is a single 0x0A newline)

The grader will:
• Check that the file exists.
• Read it with `cat -A` and expect the literal output `WEEKLY_BACKUP_TIME=03:00$` (where `$` represents the single LF). Any extra spaces, carriage returns, tabs or additional lines will cause failure.
name: task_000000_fb7cd4f2
